svg.text: Every observed one-round move in the (value, forecast-move) plane
Colored arrows are the measured round-to-round moves; the gray field is the model's predicted move from each coordinate — both are one round long.

Candidate M: observed and predicted arrows are the same object (one round, same units), so each panel is a direct observed-vs-model vector-field comparison. One arrow per consecutive round pair; judge-swap runs dashed.
Qwen risk grid — 48 round transitions from 16 runs
OLMo risk grid + judge schedules — 107 round transitions from 21 runs
OLMo mixed-pool interventions — 54 round transitions from 18 runs
oracle & injection — 33 round transitions from 11 runs
Qwen insecure-code loops — 24 round transitions from 8 runs
one observed round transition r → r+1
